section_title: INTRODUCTION AND WEEKLY OUTLOOK
content:
Welcome back. Lord willing, I'll be back next Tuesday after the holiday weekend. We'll be looking at the E
- mini S&P daily chart.

section_title: BEARISH ORDER BLOCK AND MEAN THRESHOLD
content:
We have a nice delivery to the upside. This will draw what's inside this area up into the bearish order block.
Notice we traded up into this up-close candle with today's range, didn't quite take out this high. That's the
next area to look for.
- When looking at a candle used for an order block, the low, the open, and the middle of the range are the
three sensitive areas to watch for.
- If the market trades up into the middle point of that candle, this is what I call the mean threshold.
- The mean threshold was taken today, which bodes well for a continuation to take out this short-term high.
- If you take your fib and run it across this candle, the 50 level is the mean threshold.
15
- MINUTE TIMEFRAME:

section_title: FAIR VALUE GAP AND OPTIMAL TRADE ENTRY
content:
On the 15-minute timeframe, yesterday's trading turned up into that small little area down into that low. I
mentioned that was going to be a likely candidate for upside. We also had relative equal highs. We traded
back down into a fair value gap, which was an optimal trade entry. Price rallied up, taking out buy-side
liquidity above the short-term high back here, which you can see on your own 15-minute timeframe. We
then consolidated ahead of today.

section_title: NEW YORK MIDNIGHT JUDAH SWING AND TARGET ATTAINMENT
content:
During the crossover into New York midnight, a small Judah swing occurred where price dropped down,
created the low of the day, rallied, leaving relative equal highs and this high intact. It then dropped down,
rallied once more, had a small retracement, and then did a shallow run above 4000 on this pass through.
- I used this small retracement on a lower timeframe to highlight a run to 4000, which was my objective.
- It hit 4000, and then we pressed deeper into the order block on the daily chart, creating this whole run.

section_title: CONSOLIDATION BELOW DAILY BEARISH ORDER BLOCK
content:
For now, just know that we were trading up to this level, failed to go to that same level, and consolidated
right below that daily bearish order block.
- That's the low of the candle that creates that daily bearish order block, and then the opening when that
candle is a little bit higher (around 4002, check your chart).
- We had a small pass above 4000, retraced back down, and we'll look at the lower timeframes in all of this
price action.

section_title: 5-MINUTE PRICE LEG ANALYSIS AND DEALING RANGE SELECTION
content:
Looking at the five-minute candle, this run from here to here (this price leg) is part of this larger price leg.
Notice how this candle's high to this candle's low range has one single pass, whereas this one has two
stages: low, high, small retracement, and then a second leg running.
- Because I had two stages to this delivery, I want to use this most recent one as my dealing range: the
short-term low to the short-term high.
- I ran a fib on that, as shown in the short vignette I posted on my Twitter.

section_title: ICT TWITTER HANDLE AND FRAUDULENT ACCOUNTS
content:
My Twitter handle is @IamtheICT (capital I, underscore, a m, underscore, t h e, underscore, i c t). There
are many people over there pretending to be me.
- Some have been asking for money, but I am not asking you for money.
- I am never going to ask you to DM me, and I am never going to DM you.
- If it isn't being posted publicly on Twitter, it isn't me.

section_title: DEALING RANGE, EQUILIBRIUM, AND DISCOUNT
content:
This dealing range, measured from the low to the high, has 50% as equilibrium. I want to see price drop
down into a discount. This small area down here is a fair value gap, which we will look at on a lower
timeframe.

section_title: DEVIATION FROM MODEL TIMING AND PERSONAL COMMITMENTS
content:
Some of you were asking about the times I said for the model. I'm teaching you something that is outside
the model, which is why you won't anticipate or see that typical setup. That's the whole point of why I did it
today. Plus, I have a lot of personal things to take care of and administrative work in my private group.

section_title: MARKET PROGRESSION AND KEY TIMEFRAMES
content:
With that said, the market rallies in here, drops down into the fair value gap there, once more above the
4000 level. Going into 8:30 (this is the 8:30 candle), you can watch when it trades right into that fair value
gap. It rallies again, drops back down, and then at 9:30, this candle explodes to the upside, taking off and
digging deeper into that order block. It traded up to almost the 4074 level. We'll be looking for a continuation
to the upside, with 4095 and 4100 as the next considerations.

section_title: SMART MONEY TECHNIQUE (SMT) DIVERGENCE SETUP
content:
I want to take you into the price action down here and give you more details as to why I was willing to take
something a little bit earlier today. If you plot the Nasdaq June contract for 2022 on the compare chart on
TradingView, go to the platform, click the plus symbol, add the symbol NQM2022. It will typically plot a line
chart on the close by default. You want to change that in the settings (right-click or click the gear symbol) to
plot on the low, because you're comparing lows.

section_title: IDENTIFYING SMT DIVERGENCE AND BIAS
content:
As you can see, the Nasdaq went lower here, whereas the S&P failed to go lower. That's an SMT
divergence, or Smart Money Technique. It's accumulating in here, whereas the Nasdaq is taking liquidity
out, so there's a cracking correlation between the two. Usually, they move in tandem.
- At certain times (8:30, 9:30, at news events being released), they'll create this divergence.
- If you have a bias, it's helpful. If you don't know where it's going, you'll many times encounter what would
look like SMT divergence, and then it disappears as they start moving in concert. You have to be careful
with that.
- That's why I always teach: know what it's reaching for. The bias has been bullish. For my framework, for
my setup, because I gave you 4000 and I had very little time to work with anything this morning, I just
hurried up and saw the chart. I missed the actual ideal entry. I just used a close proximity entry with an
order block.

section_title: HIGHER LOW IN S&P VS. LOWER LOW IN NASDAQ
content:
If you look at the relationship of that fair value gap, dropping back down in, a higher low than that of 8:30 in
the S&P, versus the lower low made in Nasdaq. This is a stop-out (in Nasdaq), this is accumulation of longs
(in S&P). This is going to be the telltale sign that it's going to go higher.
- If you look at what we did at 8:30, it already snapped down into this gap, and it's not likely to go lower
than that; there's no need to.
- It's being compressed, basically like a spring or a bomb. It's waiting, and then all of a sudden it explodes.
It's waiting just below that daily bearish order block and digging up into that thing at range on that up-close
candle on the daily chart. So it's climbing up, up, up, up. We watch to see if it gets to the mean threshold,
which is half of that bearish order block or up-close candle on the daily chart.

section_title: PRICE ACTION CONFIRMATION ON LOWER TIMEFRAMES
content:
Again, we can see (zoomed in) price breaks back down into the fair value gap after trading through the
order block here. This is a 15-minute timeframe order block, by the way. And then it rallies. Inside this little
area in here, there's one small little pullback into a fair value gap on the one-minute chart. I'll leave that for
you to go into and study.

section_title: MARKET STRUCTURE AND REBALANCING
content:
Here is the hourly chart. I want to kind of give you like a market structure perspective. Here's where it's
likely to be drawn to next. All of this movement down here, we completely rebalanced all that. Here is the
fair value gap I had drawn on the lower timeframe chart just a moment ago before we moved back up to an
hourly chart. We have consolidated here ahead of running all the way up into this area here.

section_title: DISCOUNT AND RALLIES
content:
Notice what was happening: we had a low, retraced down into this low-to-high discount, fair value gap,
rallies. Then dealing range low, dealing range high, retraces down into discount, rallies. Then dealing range
low, dealing range high, discount, fair value gap, order block, rally, consolidate. We rally, come back down
into the smaller timeframe fair value gap, SMT divergence, and rallies, and keeps pressing higher, higher,
higher.
- The algorithm is picking up more accumulated longs to press deeper into all of this range and likely trade
into the buy-side liquidity here.

section_title: IMPORTANCE OF BIAS AND AVOIDING COUNTER-TREND TRADING
content:
My question to you is, how many of you first didn't listen to me when I said 'don't trade,' and second, did
you try to sell short? Why? The bias has been bullish; it's likely to do what? Keep digging higher. We try not
to pick tops, and look what this has done. We've had a low, a lower low, a sudden dramatic low that we
came back up, had a shift in market structure here, then we retreated back down to this imbalance. So
we're seeing the market do what? Keep going higher, keep going higher, picking up discount levels each
time.
- You have to know what you're looking for: narrative and bias.
- Also, what in this area or over here would constitute a short? Because we have all this range in here that
it's not likely to go up to go straight all the way through all this stuff. That's a high-resistance liquidity run.
Not that it can't happen, but it's likely to not form or pan out because we had so many supporting ideas in
here.
- Down-closed candles are supporting price; it's just finding underlying order flow that is bullish.
- So it's likely to go up just to keep going higher, not go up to go down. Because to go up to go down, we
have all of these ranges that we would have to pierce and break, and that requires a lot of intent, not selling
pressure. The intent is to send this thing higher on the daily chart, which is why I took your attention to that
initial gap on the daily chart.

section_title: TRADE OBJECTIVE AND MODEL ADAPTABILITY
content:
Hopefully, you learned why today the range expansion took place and why it was not a short. We've been
bullish; we've been looking for prices to go higher. Just for clarity, 4000 was my objective only because I
mentioned 4000 during the commentaries this week. So when I was talking to you in lectures, I called this
level. I wanted to give you a point of entry and then a target hitting it. That was the whole point of me
showing you a little vignette on Twitter today, showing you what I was looking for, how to frame a trade, how
to set it up, and how you engage it, because it was setting up this trade here.
- Just for your notes, because it gave a nice run here earlier before 7:00 in the morning, that means we're
probably going to have a deep retracement and look for an ideal scenario like we outlined with the SMT
divergence.
- SMT usually will occur around a 9:30 time period when there has already been a nice early run before
7:00 or at 7:00. We got real close to 7:00 here.
- I'm not limited to this model. I know some of you were questioning, with kind of like pointed remarks on
Twitter, 'what are we doing here, why are we not using the timeframe that's used for the model?' I'm
showing you again, just proof that these things deliver as I teach them.

section_title: TEACHING METHODOLOGY AND EARLY RUN INSIGHT
content:
I have not only taught what I've revealed on YouTube; I have lots of students. I have students in my paid
group. I have casual people that come through and watch one video or two videos, and I want them to see
things that are being taught from my library of concepts.
- If I can see this setup early in the day, before 7:00 in the morning, to me it gives me insight; it gives me an
expectation around what the 9:30 opening is going to be like.
- So if I get an early run like this, my first thought is I need to go to SMT later on at 9:30 because it's
probably going to require some kind of a cracking correlation to set up the next leg, if it's going to have one.

section_title: HOLIDAY WEEKEND TRADING ADVICE AND CLOSING REMARKS
content:
It's Thursday, tomorrow's Friday. We're going into a holiday weekend. I will not be trading tomorrow; I will
not be trading on Monday.
- I'm going to be doing one video for my private group tomorrow, and it's probably going to be an early
video, not so much a commentary for price action, because it's a weekend and Memorial Day is on Monday.
- So the volume is probably going to be light on Friday. If you're going to be trading, study in the morning
session, leave the afternoon to the gamblers. Volume just probably won't be on your side in the afternoon;
everybody's going to be going away for a long weekend.
- Don't trade on Monday. Take the day off, enjoy it, have hot dogs, hamburgers, relax, do what you got to do
to be a family person. I'm going to be doing the same. Until I talk to you, Lord willing, next Tuesday, enjoy
your weekend and your holiday. Be safe.
